name: RABBIT-FEATURE-BACKLOG-10
title: rabbit-feature-touch dispatcher stops after SPEC handoff instead of continuing TDD cycle
description: Observed in ws66 during Plan E.tdd-state-machine. After the rabbit-feature-spec subagent returns its HANDOFF block to the dispatcher (the main session), the dispatcher ends its turn instead of continuing to dispatch the tdd-subagent for TEST-WRITE → IMPLEMENT → CODE-REVIEW → TEST-GREEN → UNLOCK. Stop banner confirmed HUMAN-APPROVAL-BYPASS was ON, so the bypass gate is not the cause. Workaround: nudge the dispatcher with a follow-up message like 'continue the rabbit-feature-touch cycle: dispatch tdd-subagent for TEST-WRITE...'. The dispatcher then resumes correctly. Suggests the rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md or command needs an explicit instruction along the lines of 'do not end your turn until the cycle reaches UNLOCK or you have an explicit failure to report' — otherwise the dispatcher may interpret 'delivered handoff' as natural turn boundary. May have happened in earlier Plan E waves (rabbit-feature, policy, rabbit-file) but went unnoticed because those completed without intervention. Worth investigating whether the Stop hook itself plays a role (it fires alerts on every Stop, which may signal 'turn is over' to the dispatcher).
